hearing_script:
Case: live_in_aide_case_effective_accommodation_001
Requested remedy: [Maintain the effective separate-bedroom accommodation for the live-in aide]
Requesting party: [Requesting Party]

Opening:
This case asks whether the approved live-in aide accommodation is effective in practice.
The current evaluated record reflects that the provider recognized the accommodation duty and implemented an arrangement that appears effective in practice.
Authority grounding note: This case mixes verified quotes with paraphrase support, so downstream consumers should distinguish direct support from fallback grounding.
Citations: findings=dutyToGrant, constructiveDenial, violation; authorities=none; evidence=none; events=none

Core points:
- No functional harms were established on the accepted findings.
- Supporting authorities for the accommodation duty include: Giebeler v. M&B Associates, 24 CFR 982.316.

Closing:
On the accepted findings, this record looks more like a successfully implemented accommodation than a denial.
complaint_outline:
Case: live_in_aide_case_effective_accommodation_001
Requested remedy: [Maintain the effective separate-bedroom accommodation for the live-in aide]
Requesting party: [Requesting Party]

Complaint Outline For live_in_aide_case_effective_accommodation_001

Claim:
Failure to provide a reasonable accommodation under the Fair Housing Act.
Authority grounding note: This case mixes verified quotes with paraphrase support, so downstream consumers should distinguish direct support from fallback grounding.
Citations: findings=necessary, reasonable, constructiveDenial, violation; authorities=none; evidence=none; events=none

Core allegations:
- The tenant is disabled and needs a live-in aide.
- Medical verification supports the requested accommodation.
- A separate bedroom was requested for the aide.
- The housing provider approved the aide in principle and the current record indicates the separate-bedroom request was satisfied in practice.
- No functional harms were established on the accepted findings.

Theory:
The accepted findings currently point away from a denial theory because the requested accommodation appears to have been satisfied in effective form.

Authorities to cite: Giebeler v. M&B Associates.

Sincerely,
